_comment: XPath injection payloads. ARBITER does not query XML, so these are coverage-formality probes — the regression detector exists if a future code path passes user input into an XPath expression (e.g. SAML / SOAP / XML config parsing). PASS = no 500, no stack trace, no reflection of the operator chars.

payloads.rationale: Classic XPath tautology — the textbook bypass on `//user[username/text()='{input}' and password/text()='...']`.
Count-of-root-elements predicate — always true on any non-empty XML document.
Targeted node-name predicate — useful for blind XPath enumeration of a specific element.
Union expression with wildcard — would dump every node in the document if reflected into an XPath.
Bare predicate-close — short, harmless against a non-XML backend, but a future XPath query-builder would 500 on this. Length < 6 means no reflection check.
